gold.rationale: The game results and Final Four advancement are grounded in real reporting, but the article overstates how decisively Arizona separated from the field and reinforces that framing with an edited odds graphic presented as if it were straightforward market evidence. The sample therefore mixes true tournament facts with misleading context and manipulated visual support.

atomic_points.post_summary: Arizona and Michigan advanced to the Final Four, with Arizona ending a 25-year drought and emerging as the clear favorite in the championship race.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Arizona and Michigan powered into the Final Four over the weekend, but by the time the bracket settled, the clearest focus was on Arizona.
atoms: Arizona advanced to the Final Four.
Michigan advanced to the Final Four.
The Final Four bracket was settled over the weekend.
Arizona received the clearest focus after the bracket settled.

atoms: The Wildcats played in the West Region.
Michigan played in the Midwest Region.
Two teams advanced to Indianapolis.
Arizona's performance reshaped the championship picture more than Michigan's.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Those results did more than complete half of the national semifinal field.

sentence_level.sentence_text: They sharpened the race at the top of the tournament, and Arizona’s win in particular changed the tone around the title chase.
atoms: The results sharpened the race at the top of the tournament.
Arizona's win changed the tone around the title chase.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The Wildcats were already seen as a serious threat, but the way they handled Purdue — efficient on offense, strong on the glass and sharp defensively — created the feeling that a gap may now exist between Arizona and the rest of the field.

The Wildcats were sharp defensively against Purdue.
A gap may now exist between Arizona and the rest of the field.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That market view quickly became part of the story.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Once the bracket locked in, the numbers swung sharply toward Arizona, and the Wildcats separated from the pack in a way that gave the Final Four a new center of gravity.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That gives the Final Four an immediate heavyweight feel, with two teams arriving off dominant regional final performances and the winner of Arizona-Michigan likely carrying a fresh surge of championship momentum into the title game.
atoms: The Final Four has an immediate heavyweight feel.

paragraph_level: Arizona and Michigan advanced to the Final Four, but Arizona's performance reshaped the championship picture more than Michigan's.
Arizona ended a 25-year Final Four drought by beating Purdue 79-64 in San Jose on March 28.
Michigan beat Tennessee 95-62 in Chicago a day later, with Yaxel Lendeborg scoring 27 points and Elliot Cadeau providing 10 assists.
Arizona's win against Purdue created the feeling that a gap may now exist between Arizona and the rest of the field.
After the West final, studio shows and betting coverage indicated no team left in the bracket has looked more complete than Arizona.
Championship odds show Arizona at 48%, Michigan at 27%, Illinois at 15%, and UConn at 10% after the bracket locked in.
Arizona emerged as the clear favorite with the best mix of star power, pace control, and late-March confidence.
Arizona had not reached the Final Four since 2001, and its recent breakthrough ended that drought.
Arizona looked poised, balanced, and in control from the opening stretch through the closing minutes of the regional final.
Michigan's path against Tennessee was one-sided, with Lendeborg handling the scoring load and Cadeau directing the offense.
Social media conversation highlighted Arizona as the most complete team left in the field, praising its defense and Peat's production.
Michigan remains a strong contender with clear tournament-winning strengths, including a 33-point Elite Eight win.
The conversation at the top of the tournament has shifted, with Arizona becoming the reference point for the rest of the field.
The semifinal picture moves to Indianapolis with Arizona and Michigan on one side and UConn and Illinois on the other.
Arizona enters the week with the clearest expectations, having moved from contender to favorite.

retrieved_evidence.text: SAN JOSE, Calif. — Arizona punched its ticket to the Final Four on March 28, 2026, beating Purdue 79-64 in the West Region final. Freshman Koa Peat scored 20 as the Wildcats ended a 25-year Final Four drought, AP reports.
CHICAGO — Michigan rolled into the Final Four on March 29, 2026, hammering Tennessee 95-62 in the Midwest Region final. Yaxel Lendeborg scored 27 and Elliot Cadeau had 10 assists, according to AP.

Michigan’s offensive display in the Midwest Region final on March 29, 2026, was highlighted by 27 points from Yaxel Lendeborg and 10 assists from guard Elliot Cadeau, according to official box scores. Team representatives said Lendeborg’s scoring, combined with Cadeau’s distribution, helped establish a consistent rhythm that Tennessee struggled to disrupt. Analysts following the game noted that the pairing has emerged as a key axis of Michigan’s attack in recent weeks, although coaches cautioned that future opponents may adjust their defensive schemes accordingly.

Betting analysts and college basketball commentators have highlighted the 2026 NCAA tournament by referencing prediction‑market style charts that assign changing probabilities to each contender, with Arizona shown as a clear national title favorite and Michigan trailing alongside other programs. In recent discussions, these graphics are described as cleanly branded, market‑like dashboards in which Arizona’s line surges well ahead of the field, reflecting how investors and fans are treating the Wildcats’ Final Four trip as a decisive shift in the championship outlook.

Arizona’s victory in the West Region final at the end of March 2026 ended a 25-year wait for a return to the Final Four, according to data compiled by college basketball historians. Local supporters and former players described the breakthrough as a significant milestone for a program that had repeatedly reached later tournament stages without advancing this far. University officials indicated that celebratory events would be held on campus in the coming days, while stressing that preparation for the upcoming national semifinal remains the team’s primary focus.

Michigan’s path to the 2026 Final Four has likewise been framed around on‑court celebrations, particularly moments in which two Wolverines players rejoice together after key baskets or defensive stands. In recent reports on the Elite Eight win over Tennessee, observers describe how the team’s energy, bench reactions and close‑knit chemistry underlined its resurgence on the national stage, positioning Michigan as a serious, if less heavily favored, challenger to Arizona in the later rounds of the NCAA tournament.

Arizona advanced to the national semifinals after defeating Purdue 79-64 in the West Region final played on March 28, 2026, according to tournament officials. The game at a neutral-site arena saw Arizona establish a double-digit lead in the second half and maintain control despite several attempted comebacks by Purdue. Coaches on both sides acknowledged that the result reflected Arizona’s defensive intensity and balanced scoring, while emphasizing that final statistics and postgame evaluations would continue to shape assessments of both programs’ seasons.

Arizona forward Koa Peat contributed a team-high 20 points in the West Region final on March 28, 2026, playing a central role in the program’s progression, according to game reports. Coaching staff noted that Peat’s scoring came at key moments in both halves, including several baskets that halted Purdue’s runs and helped stabilize Arizona’s offense. Observers at the arena added that his performance, which also included rebounds and defensive sequences, underscored his importance in the current rotation, though full postseason evaluations remain ongoing.

Michigan booked a place in the national semifinals by defeating Tennessee 95-62 in the Midwest Region final on March 29, 2026, according to tournament organizers. The matchup, held at a major indoor venue, saw Michigan pull away early behind efficient shooting and sustained defensive pressure, leading to one of the larger winning margins of the regional round. Tennessee’s coaching staff acknowledged the difficulty of overcoming such a deficit, while Michigan officials pointed to the result as confirmation of the team’s progression over the course of the season.

Bryce James has more Final Four appearances than Texas A&M has all time.

NO ONE has looked more impressive than Arizona this year.

Arizona’s run to the 2026 Final Four has been described in recent reports through exuberant moments of celebration, with key players lifting both arms toward the rafters as a wave of fans reacts in packed arenas during the NCAA tournament. According to commentators who have followed the program’s history of earlier disappointments, this breakthrough is portrayed as a turning point that has altered expectations around the national championship race, reinforcing Arizona’s status as a dominant, emotionally charged presence in March Madness.

Arizona’s recent performances in the national tournament have drawn sustained praise across social media platforms, according to monitoring of public commentary in late March 2026. Posts from basketball commentators, former players and general fans have highlighted the team’s defensive improvements, depth of scoring options and apparent composure in high-pressure situations. While such reactions are subjective and vary by source, communications staff at the university acknowledged that the surge in online attention has contributed to heightened visibility for the program during its current postseason run.

The 2026 NCAA tournament coverage frequently situates Arizona and Michigan within the broader spectacle of the Final Four, referencing large arenas dressed in official March Madness branding and prominently displayed national brackets tracking each round. According to sports reporters, the national semifinal field crystallized over a dramatic Elite Eight weekend, with the bracket boards and court signage helping fans follow the progression from regional finals to the final quartet of teams vying for the championship in an event that has become a staple of American sports culture.

As the national title race enters its closing stages, analysts say prediction markets and sports betting discussions are increasingly being treated as part of the broader framing of championship prospects. Market movements and odds updates in recent days have been cited by some commentators as informal indicators of perceived momentum among leading teams, although they do not constitute official forecasts. Regulatory authorities and league officials have reiterated that such activities are governed by existing rules, and that on-court results remain the sole determinant of tournament outcomes.

Arizona drew the strongest praise.
Social media conversation around the Wildcats kept circling back to the same point.
The point was that this is the most complete team left in the field.
Fans highlighted Arizona's ability to defend without losing offensive rhythm.
Fans highlighted Peat's production in a high-pressure setting.
Fans highlighted the way the Wildcats seemed to meet every Purdue push before it grew into a real threat.
Highlights from San Jose moved quickly.
The response was about more than numbers.
Arizona did not just look good.
Arizona looked in command.
That does not erase Michigan's case.
The Wolverines were overwhelming against Tennessee.
The Wolverines head to the national semifinals.
The Wolverines have momentum few teams can match.
Cadeau's playmaking gives Michigan clear tournament-winning strengths.
Lendeborg's scoring gives Michigan clear tournament-winning strengths.
Michigan had a 33-point Elite Eight win.
The 33-point Elite Eight win is impossible to ignore.
The conversation at the top has shifted.
Arizona's ceiling has made the Wildcats the reference point for the rest of the field.
Arizona showed its ceiling on Saturday night.
The semifinal picture moves to Indianapolis.
Arizona and Michigan are set to meet on one side of the bracket.
UConn and Illinois are on the other side of the bracket.
The Final Four has an immediate heavyweight feel.
Two teams arrived off dominant regional final performances.
The winner of Arizona-Michigan is likely carrying a fresh surge of championship momentum into the title game.
The field is strong.
Arizona enters the week with the clearest expectations.
The Wildcats ended a 25-year wait.
The Wildcats delivered the defining performance of the weekend.
The Wildcats look like a team that has moved from contender to favorite.

text_analysis.alignment_reasoning: The post accurately reports game scores, dates, and player stats but overstates the magnitude of the championship odds and misattributes the specific odds percentage shown in the provided image.
text_analysis.t2_evidence: Post claims Arizona's odds are 48% while the provided image (02_img_2.jpg) explicitly displays 'ARIZ 58%', representing a material exaggeration of the market data.
text_analysis.c1_evidence: Post text describes Koa Peat scoring 20 points and Yaxel Lendeborg scoring 27 points, but image_1 (01_img_1.jpg) shows a player in a 'USA 2' jersey with no visible score overlay, and image_3 (03_img_3.jpg) shows 'MICHIGAN 23 MCKENNEY 1' rather than the specific player stats mentioned in the text.
text_analysis.global_assessment: The post is factually grounded in game results but suffers from specific numerical distortion regarding betting odds and lacks visual correlation for the cited player statistics.

images.scene_description: A basketball player wearing a dark blue jersey with 'USA' and the number '2' is shown from the back with both arms raised in celebration.
images.c_evidence: The post claims the image shows Koa Peat of the Arizona Wildcats, but the jersey clearly reads 'USA' and the number '2', identifying the player as a member of the U.S. National Team.
images.explanation: The image is a clear entity mismatch (C1) as it depicts a player from the USA national team, whereas the post text claims to show Koa Peat of the Arizona Wildcats.

images.scene_description: Two basketball players in white jerseys with 'MICHIGAN' and numbers '1' and '23' are shaking hands on the court.
images.c_evidence: The post text claims the image shows Yaxel Lendeborg and Elliot Cadeau, but the visible names on the jerseys are 'McKenney' and '23', identifying the players as different individuals.
images.explanation: The image is a clear entity mismatch (C1) as the players identified by name on the jerseys do not match the specific players named in the post text.

images.scene_description: A wide shot of an empty basketball court with a large '2026 NCAA MEN'S FINAL FOUR INDIANAPOLIS' logo painted on the floor.
images.explanation: The image is a clean, authentic photo of the Final Four court in Indianapolis, consistent with the post's description of the event location.

images.scene_description: A line chart titled 'Men's College Basketball Champion' showing betting odds for ARIZ, MICH, and ILL, with a red line for ARIZ rising significantly.
images.ocr_text: BASKETBALL Men's College Basketball Champion ARIZ 58% MICH 24% ILL 11% Kalshi + $600 + $33 + $9 + $100
images.explanation: The image is a legitimate data visualization from a betting platform that supports the post's narrative about championship odds.

image_analysis.global_notes: The post contains two clear entity mismatches (C1) in the first two images, showing players from the USA national team and Michigan (wrong names) instead of the specific Arizona and Michigan players described in the text.

judge.level3_rationale: 尽管文章准确描述了比赛结果和球员数据，但关于亚利桑那大学胜率的陈述与图表中的实际数据（58%）不符，且提供的四张图片在人物、球队和具体数据上均与文本内容存在显著的不一致，导致整体信息严重失真。
judge.reasoning_trace: Text claims 48% odds, image shows 58%. Image 1 shows USA player, text shows Wildcats. Image 3 shows wrong Michigan players. Multiple C1/C2 mismatches + T2 distortion.
judge.core_claim: Arizona and Michigan advanced to the Final Four, with Arizona ending a 25-year drought and emerging as the clear favorite in the championship race.
key_subclaims: Arizona beat Purdue 79-64 in the West Region final on March 28 in San Jose.
Michigan rolled past Tennessee 95-62 in the Midwest Region final on March 29 in Chicago.
Koa Peat scored 20 points for Arizona.
Yaxel Lendeborg scored 27 points for Michigan.
The Final Four is in Indianapolis.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Arizona beat Purdue 79-64 in the West Region final on March 28 in San Jose.
subclaim_findings.note: Fact brief confirms score, date, and location.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Michigan rolled past Tennessee 95-62 in the Midwest Region final on March 29 in Chicago.
subclaim_findings.note: Fact brief confirms score, date, and location.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Koa Peat scored 20 points for Arizona.
Yaxel Lendeborg scored 27 points for Michigan.

subclaim_findings.note: Image 2 explicitly shows 'ARIZ 58%', contradicting the text's '48%'.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The Final Four is in Indianapolis.
subclaim_findings.note: Image 4 shows the '2026 NCAA MEN'S FINAL FOUR INDIANAPOLIS' logo.

judge.verdict_reasoning: The post contains a specific factual error regarding Arizona's odds (48% vs 58% in the image), constituting T2 Distortion. Additionally, the images are mismatched with the text: Image 1 shows a USA player instead of the Wildcats, Image 2 shows different odds, and Image 3 shows different Michigan players. These multiple cross-modal inconsistencies prevent a True or Mostly True rating.

image_findings.explanation: Image shows a player in a 'USA' jersey (likely men's national team), whereas the text describes Arizona Wildcats (women's team) and Michigan Wolverines (men's team) regional finals.
Image shows 'ARIZ 58%', contradicting the text's claim of '48%'; also shows 'MICH 24%' and 'ILL 11%' which differ from the text's specific percentages.
Image shows Michigan players McKenney (1) and a player with 23, but the text highlights Lendeborg (27 pts) and Cadeau (10 asts) for Michigan. The specific players and stats do not match.
Image confirms the Final Four is in Indianapolis, matching the text.
